note: My feature: add opt-in debug/trace logging to StreamListener in streaming_listener.py. This is the only file I need to touch. What file(s) does your feature need?

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Correction: my actual feature is opt-in DEBUG TRACE LOGGING for StreamListener (not is_last_chunk - that must be a third agent's feature). Since both our features live inside StreamListener, the file is unavoidably shared - there's no other place to put it. To minimize collision risk, I'll keep my edits purely additive/localized: (1) new __init__ params 'debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None' appended at the END of the param list, after your idle_timeout_s if you add one first; (2) 'self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger("dspy.streaming.listener")' added at end of __init__ body; (3) guarded 'if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):' log lines inserted around start-detection, rolling-end-check, emit-chunk, flush, and the allow_reuse reset block in receive()/flush() - I will NOT rewrite any existing line, only insert new ones. Can you confirm whether your tick()/heartbeat logic also touches the allow_reuse reset block inside receive(), so we order our insertions without stepping on the same lines? Once confirmed I'll ACCEPT and start.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - you go first with idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat (new __init__ params, timestamp refresh line in receive(), new tick() method). I'll wait for your DONE, then layer my debug/trace logging additively on top (new debug/debug_logger __init__ params, self._logger setup, guarded log lines at start-detection/rolling-end-check/emit-chunk/flush/reset points) without touching your heartbeat code. Standing by.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished debug/trace logging feature. Added debug: bool=False and debug_logger: logging.Logger|None=None params to __init__ (after allow_reuse), self._logger setup, and guarded 'if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):' DEBUG-level log calls at: (1) both stream_start=True assignment sites (cache_hit branch and main queue branch) - 'Start detection: adapter=X, field=Y, stream_start=True, buffer_preview=Z'; (2) the regex end-match inside the stream_start block - 'Rolling end check: ... reason=regex_match, buffered_size=N'; (3) right before the StreamResponse return in receive() - 'Emit chunk: len(token)=N, queue_size=N, is_last_chunk=False'; (4) end of flush() (converted its 3 early-returns to a single result var + return so the log fires once) - 'Flush: ... truncated_buffer_length=N'; (5) inside the existing allow_reuse reset block - 'State reset for field X (allow_reuse=True)'. Added a module-level _truncate_for_log() helper (80-char cap + '...'). All existing tests/streaming pass, ruff clean, verified debug=False path is a no-op. I did not touch your idle_timeout_s/tick() additions or flush()'s existing branch logic beyond the trivial return->result refactor. Ready to submit patch.txt whenever you are.
